Schaerbeek cherries, which had gone by the wayside for a few decades because they were so rare and difficult to harvest, have been rediscovered by lambic breweries such as the Timmermans brewery because of its bright red juice and tart-sweet taste. We give these Schaerbeek cherries six months to macerate in one of our young lambics. We then mix this cherry lambic with a lambic that has aged for 19 weeks in bourbon barrels. The final touch? The mixed fermentation is what makes this beer so special. The top-fermented brown ale is subtly blended with the best spontaneously fermented lambic, which is then matured in oak barrels for many months. It is this process that gives the red-brown beer a rich, creamy finish."
About the style: Even though "Bruin "suggests a style like "Old Brown "or "Brown Ale ", this beer should definitively be classified as Flanders Red Ale (Category 23B in BJCP 2015), because its aromas and flavors are rather fruity than malty, it has mixed fermentation, it 's aged in oak casks and its brewing location is in West Flanders.","image":"https://labels.untappd.com/561371","abv":"5.0","untappd_style":"Sour - Flanders Red Ale","untappd_rating":"3.52005","wab_location":null,"lager":"","pale":"","ipa":"","dark":"","sour":"Yes","wheat":"","farmhouse":"","cask":"","pastry":"","belgian":"Yes","strong":"","fruited":"","barrel":"","cider":"","gf":"","non_alc":"","other":"","fri_am":"Yes","fri_pm":"Yes","sat_am":"Yes","sat_pm":"Yes","festival_rating_count":0,"festival_average_rating":0},"2832165":{"beer_name":"Lambic & Stout","brewer_name":"Brewery John Martin & Brewery Timmermans","wab_beer_id":2832165,"wab_brewer_id":2313,"ut_bid":2832165,"untappd_brewery_id":2313,"description":"Guinness & Timmermans created an amazing blend of Guinness Special Export, also called Antwerpen Stout, with Timmermans Oude Kriek from the world’s oldest lambic brewery.

One that is hyper hazy and thick. This is a that fits that modern milkshake style approach to IPAs.

Using Oats and Wheat, in tandem with Talus and Ekuanot hops, this is a beer that brings aromas of fruit and booze, as well as flavours of peach and passionfruit.

It is said that whistling at sea brings bad luck to fishermen. The simple act of pursing your lips and chirping out a tune can summon up storms and serpents from the deep. Superstitions like these are still respected today, they are ways of working passed down from generation to generation. Brewed with a vibrant dry hop blend of Citra and Simcoe, it bursts with succulent aromas of ripe mango, juicy peach, and zesty grapefruit, balanced by subtle pine and floral undertones.

With it 's gentle carbonation it lifts the tropical fruit-forward character, leaving with just an edge of bitterness, making it endlessly drinkable and refreshingly modern.

Slipway is more than just a beer - it’s a nod to the historic slipways that line Cornwall’s rugged coastline. These sloped ramps, once used to launch fishing boats into the Atlantic, are symbols of the region’s deep maritime roots. Just like those slipways, our session beer is just the beginning of an adventure.

Slipway is your taste of the Cornish coast in a glass.","image":"https://labels.untappd.com/4268883","abv":"5.0","untappd_style":"IPA - Session New England / Hazy","untappd_rating":"3.73956","wab_location":null,"lager":"","pale":"","ipa":"","dark":"","sour":"","wheat":"","farmhouse":"","cask":"","pastry":"","belgian":"","strong":"","fruited":"","barrel":"","cider":"","gf":"","non_alc":"","other":"Yes","fri_am":"Yes","fri_pm":"Yes","sat_am":"Yes","sat_pm":"Yes","festival_rating_count":0,"festival_average_rating":0},"6175894":{"beer_name":"Drift","brewer_name":"St Ives Brewery","wab_beer_id":6175894,"wab_brewer_id":22128,"ut_bid":6175894,"untappd_brewery_id":22128,"description":"","image":"https://labels.untappd.com/6175894","abv":"4.0","untappd_style":"Bitter - Session / Ordinary","untappd_rating":"3.54984","wab_location":null,"lager":"","pale":"","ipa":"","dark":"","sour":"","wheat":"","farmhouse":"","cask":"Yes","pastry":"","belgian":"","strong":"","fruited":"","barrel":"","cider":"","gf":"","non_alc":"","other":"","fri_am":"Yes","fri_pm":"Yes","sat_am":"Yes","sat_pm":"Yes","festival_rating_count":0,"festival_average_rating":0},"6735956":{"beer_name":"Muddly Talker","brewer_name":"Apex Brewing Co. A true northern German Style Pilsner. Brewing great Pils is about paying attention to all of the small details and being meticulous in process. To that extent we start with 100% reverse osmosis water to allow a water profile perfect for brewing this style. Adding in only the necessary minerals allows us to achieve the crisp and soft palate that a beer like this should have. Then we use 100% Pilsner malt from Bamberg to give gentle bready notes. Hop selection sees a trifecta of absolutely stellar German hops. Two classic noble hops; Hallertau Mittelfruh and Tettnang provide beautiful herbal, floral and slight lemon zest notes which are perfectly complimented by a more modern German variety; Saphir. A delightful hop that marries some classic noble like characteristics with zingy tangerine notes. While not dry hopped, we pack the whirlpool full of these hops so this beer is not short on hop character. The beer is cast away to fermentation where it meets our house bavarian lager yeast. We ferment this as low as possible for a long slow fermentation that yields an extremely clean and crisp pils once the extended lagering process is complete. Towards the end of fermentation the beer gets spunded to achieve an elegant carbonation.","image":"https://labels.untappd.com/4235873","abv":"5.0","untappd_style":"Pilsner - German","untappd_rating":"3.45858","wab_location":null,"lager":"Yes","pale":"","ipa":"","dark":"","sour":"","wheat":"","farmhouse":"","cask":"","pastry":"","belgian":"","strong":"","fruited":"","barrel":"","cider":"","gf":"","non_alc":"","other":"","fri_am":"Yes","fri_pm":"Yes","sat_am":"Yes","sat_pm":"Yes","festival_rating_count":0,"festival_average_rating":0},"4318412":{"beer_name":"SANTAKO","brewer_name":"Garage Beer Co.","wab_beer_id":4318412,"wab_brewer_id":182609,"ut_bid":4318412,"untappd_brewery_id":182609,"description":"Our newest West Coast IPA to come off the line is Santako. The premium dark lager has complemented Budweiser Budvar Brewery’s traditional product range. It is brewed in the same way as the premium pale lager, using first-class Saaz hops, Moravian malt, water drawn from 300-metre deep artesian wells and three types of special colour malted barley: Munich, caramel and roasted.

The beer is characterised by a distinctly dark colour, a dry and mildly bitter caramel flavour without any dominant sweetness and a delicious aroma of roasted malt.

Prémiový tmavý ležák doplňuje tradiční nabídku piva z pivovaru Budějovický Budvar, n. p. od roku 2004. A 750-year long tradition of brewing the original Beer from Budweis and a unique 90-day long maturing period amplify the beer’s exceptionality even more. Budweiser Budvar Premium Pale Lager can thus be savoured with all the senses. Firstly, your eye can see its beautiful golden colour and rich thick head, then you can smell its mild hop aroma and feel the misted glass in your palm, and finally use your taste buds to savour its mild medium-strong bitterness. Our premium pale lager will forever impress in your mind.

This unique, beautiful brew is often imitated but never matched and is sold literally all over the world.

With countless awards to its credit, it is something of which every Briton can be very proud and is the epitome of the greatest of British brewing tradition.

In the early years of the modern brewing era, about two hundred years ago, many brewers produced a dark, strong ‘stock’ beer in the winter months, to provide a base amount of fermented beer to add to beers brewed in the rather more volatile months of the summer.

Old Peculier probably owes its origins to this. The name pays tribute to the unique ecclesiastical status of Masham as a ‘Court of the Peculier’ and is also reference to the strong characteristic of the beer! This 7% ABV West Coast IPA is a masterclass in hop intensity, loaded with Simcoe, Mosaic, and Strata across the boil, whirlpool, and dry-hop, plus a cheeky dose of Simcoe Dynaboost in the fermenter. The result? A wave of resinous pine, zesty citrus, and tropical brightness that crashes across your palate with precision and attitude.

It pours clear golden and dangerously inviting, with a firm bitterness that makes every sip thrilling.
